coordinate_system.primary_axis: Z axis is the gear rotational axis and the Ø9 H7 bore axis.
coordinate_system.orientation_notes: +Z is the right axial face identified as datum A; -Z is the opposite left axial face. Radial direction is outward to the external helical teeth. The axial section view is drawn with Z horizontally and radius vertically.
coordinate_system.datum_origin: Not explicitly declared; practical reference inferred at the intersection of the Ø9 H7 bore axis with datum face A.

features.name: Cylindrical gear body
features.description: Overall finished cylindrical body carrying external helical gear teeth, one central through bore, two axial end faces, and specified edge breaks. Envelope is approximately Ø39.79 mm by 14 mm wide before subtracting the Ø9 mm bore.

geometry.position_description: Axisymmetric gear body centred on the bore/gear axis.

features.material_condition: Case-hardened and tempered per part-level heat treatment note.

features.name: External left-hand helical gear tooth form
features.description: External helical gear surface: z=39 teeth, normal module m=1 mm, pitch diameter d=40.376 mm, base diameter db=37.783 mm, generated root circle diameter df=35.487 -0.08 mm, effective root diameter about 37.858 -0.03 mm, DIN 3972/II basic rack tooth profile, pressure angle 20°, change in head height k=-0.18 mm, helix angle β=15°, left-rising flank direction, addendum modification coefficient x*=-1.134, DIN 58405 / 8d quality. Drawing note indicates the head is milled over during toothing.

geometry.position_description: External tooth form around full circumference, coaxial with Ø9 H7 bore.

surface_finish.notes: Local surface texture symbol Rz 6 near the tooth/external surface; Rz is not Ra.

features.material_condition: Case-hardened gear tooth surface per heat-treatment note.

relationships.detail: The cylindrical body and external helical gear teeth share the same rotational axis.
The external helical gear tooth form is centred on the body axis.
The part body is centred on the central through bore axis.
The Ø9 H7 bore lies on the body/gear rotational axis.
The helical gear teeth and the Ø9 H7 bore share a common gear axis.
The central bore axis is the same axis as the external gear tooth form.
Datum face A is the +Z axial face opposite the left -Z axial face.
The left axial face is opposite datum face A across the 14 mm gear width.
The Ø9 H7 bore axis is normal to datum face A.
The Ø9 H7 bore axis is normal to the opposite axial face.
Datum face A bounds the right end of the cylindrical gear body.
The left axial face bounds the left end of the cylindrical gear body.
The through bore passes through the full body width.
The OD axial edge chamfers modify the edges of the external tooth/outer diameter surface.
One external edge chamfer modifies the junction of the OD with datum face A.
One external edge chamfer modifies the junction of the OD with the left axial face.
The bore entry chamfers modify both mouths of the Ø9 H7 through bore.
The +Z bore entry chamfer lies at datum face A.
The -Z bore entry chamfer lies at the left axial face.
The runout tolerance on the external gear/OD feature references datum A.
The general edge-break condition applies to unspecified sharp edges of the overall gear body.
Unspecified sharp edges at bore mouths are subject to the general deburr condition except where the specific bore chamfers apply.
[auto-enriched] Both F006 and F007 (chamfer) are accessed from the axial face

datums.description: Datum A is the right axial face of the gear, identified by a datum feature symbol attached to that face in the axial section.

datum_scheme.datum_hierarchy_notes: Only datum A is clearly identified. It is used by the 0.035 runout control. The drawing also contains no section cut labels that conflict with datum A.

feature_graph.graph_summary: The part is a small external left-hand helical spur gear, drawing C.14.373-00, made from 16MnCrS5 1.7139 and case-hardened/tempered. The finished envelope is approximately Ø39.79 mm by 14 mm wide, with 39 external helical teeth, normal module 1 mm, 15° helix angle, 20° pressure angle, DIN 3972/II tooth profile, and DIN 58405 / 8d gear quality. A central Ø9 H7 bore passes through the full width and is coaxial with the gear tooth form. The +Z axial end face is datum A, and the external gear/OD has a 0.035 runout tolerance relative to datum A. Both OD axial edges have 0.3 x 45° chamfers, and the bore entrances appear to have 0.7 mm / 30° chamfers. General notes specify Rz25 general surface texture, deburring/sharp edge removal, DIN ISO 2768-mK general tolerances, and RoHS/REACH compliance.

clarifications.question: The gear data table row for tooth tip rounding appears to show a question mark for 'Kopfrundung / tooth tip rounding'. What is the required tooth tip rounding value?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The exact tooth tip/root rounding affects the finished gear tooth profile and inspection criteria.

clarifications.question: Please confirm whether the local Rz 2.5 surface texture callout applies to the Ø9 H7 bore or to the adjacent axial datum face.
clarifications.why_it_matters: It determines which surface has the fine finish requirement; the leader is visually close to both the bore region and the datum face.
clarifications.suggested_default: Apply Rz 2.5 to the Ø9 H7 bore.

clarifications.question: Please confirm that the 0.7 and 30° callouts describe matching chamfers at both mouths of the Ø9 H7 bore.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The bore entry geometry is visible in section but the exact meaning of the 0.7 dimension is not fully legible.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as 2X bore entry chamfers, 0.7 mm at 30°.

clarifications.question: Please confirm the unilateral tolerance on the outside/head diameter is Ø39.79 +0/-0.05 mm.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The small superscript/subscript tolerance next to the diameter is legible enough for a best extraction but not perfectly clear.

guardrail_results.message: Title block and part-level data were extracted before feature extraction: drawing number, material, scale, general tolerance standard, heat treatment and notes captured.
No ordinate dimensioning was present. Envelope was derived from the maximum visible OD Ø39.79 and axial width 14 mm, not from gear theoretical pitch diameter.
Full scan found no metric thread callouts such as M2 through M12; no thread feature nodes are required.
Only one diameter hole/bore callout was identified: Ø9 H7 central bore; it has been extracted as F003. No hole was created from a non-Ø ordinate or table value.
The only hole/bore is through the 14 mm gear width; no slots, pockets or blind holes are present. Ambiguous chamfer dimensions were flagged for clarification.
Datum A was identified from a boxed datum feature symbol attached to the right axial face. No section-cut labels were confused with datums.
All feature nodes have at least one spatial relationship; symmetric coaxial and opposite-face relationships were added in both directions.
Low-to-moderate confidence items were re-read and flagged: OD unilateral tolerance, bore chamfer 0.7/30°, Rz2.5 leader target, and tooth tip rounding table entry.

analysis_trace.summary: Single DIN A4 sheet, primary axial sectional view on the left, pictorial/isometric gear view at upper right, gear data table at right, and title block/general notes at bottom.
issues: Some small superscript/subscript tolerances and gear table entries are difficult to read at the supplied rendering size.

analysis_trace.summary: Identified the left view as an axial section through the gear axis. Identified the upper-right view as a 3D isometric confirmation view. The right table is a gear specification table, not a projection view.

Extracted the cylindrical gear body, external helical gear tooth feature, central Ø9 H7 through bore, two axial faces including datum A, OD chamfers, bore entry chamfers, and general edge-break/deburr condition.
issues: No dedicated gear feature type exists in the schema, so the gear teeth were encoded as an external_cylinder with embedded gear specs.

analysis_trace.summary: Correlated the bore seen in the axial section with the hole visible in the isometric view as one through bore. Correlated the external toothed OD in the isometric view with the axial-section OD and gear data table as one external gear feature.
Built a coaxial axis graph among body, external gear teeth, and central bore; added opposite-face relationship between the two axial faces; linked chamfers and edge breaks to their parent surfaces.
Embedded Ø9 H7, Ø39.79 +0/-0.05, 14 mm width, 0.3 x 45° chamfers, 0.7/30° bore chamfers, gear table values, and the 0.035 runout to datum A into the relevant feature nodes.
issues: The tooth tip rounding row appears to contain an unresolved '?' value and is raised as clarification C001.

analysis_trace.summary: Detected datum A from the boxed letter with datum triangle on the right axial face; associated the runout control frame with datum A.
Extracted material 16MnCrS5 1.7139, case-hardening/tempering note, general Rz25 finish block, DIN ISO 2768-mK general tolerances, deburr note, and RoHS/REACH compliance note.
issues: Surface field in title block appears blank; surface texture requirements are instead shown in the lower-left surface texture symbol block.

analysis_trace.summary: Thread completeness check found no M-thread callouts. Hole check found only Ø9 H7. Depth check set bore through-depth to 14 mm. Re-read ambiguous low-confidence values and created clarifications. Relationship check confirmed no orphan features and symmetric relationships for coaxial/opposite-face pairs.
issues: Rz2.5 leader and bore chamfer details remain somewhat ambiguous but best-guess values are included with clarification questions.
